target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B-stage AI & Software company with a current post-money valuation of $1.0B and $155M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparator (Palantir), with same-sector, same-stage companies including Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, and Photoroom.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money and applying a typical 1.9x Series B advancement multiplier, implies $1.9B. Peer-median methodology across the four direct comparators yields a median valuation of $5.3B, though the peer set is notably dispersed: ranging from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence), reflecting substantial variance in stage-equivalent AI company valuations.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $1.6B to $6.1B, with a median implication of $3.6B. The material gap between the stage-progression result ($1.9B) and peer-median ($5.3B) reflects the outsized valuations assigned to frontier AI companies in the current market, particularly relative to traditional stage-progression benchmarks. Key uncertainties include whether Nominal's positioning and momentum align with high-valuation peers (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence) or mid-market comparators, and whether the peer set itself represents sustainable or bubble-era multiples for the sector.
